description: Machine-readable representation of UK Child Benefit eligibility criteria - Enhanced with Phase 1 & Phase 2 improvements

has_qualifying_child.description: Determine child's age category for eligibility
has_qualifying_child.routing_logic: Under 16: automatic qualification. 16-19: check education/training. 20+: not eligible

check_education_or_extension.description: For 16-19 year olds, check if in education/training or eligible for extension
check_education_or_extension.routing_logic: If in education/training, validate type and level. If just left education (within 20 weeks), check extension eligibility.

check_education_level.question: Is the child in approved (non-advanced) education or training?
check_education_level.help_text: Approved education: A-levels, T-levels, NVQ Level 3 or below, Scottish Highers/Advanced Highers. Advanced (NOT approved): University degrees, HNC, HND, NVQ Level 4+. Must be full-time (12+ hours/week supervised study).

criteria.must_not_be_married_unless_dependent: Married 16-19 year olds only qualify if financially dependent on claimant

check_extension_eligibility.question: Has the child (aged 16-17) left education within the last 20 weeks AND registered with a qualifying service?
check_extension_eligibility.help_text: 20-week extension available if child has left approved education/training and registered with: government careers service, Connexions, or armed services. Extension ends at week 20 or when child turns 18, whichever is sooner.

check_child_not_working_full_time.question: Is the child working less than 24 hours per week (or not in paid work)?
check_child_not_working_full_time.help_text: Children aged 16+ working 24+ hours per week and not in approved education/training are not eligible

check_child_not_receiving_benefits.question: Is the child NOT receiving certain benefits in their own right (e.g., ESA, Universal Credit)?
check_child_not_receiving_benefits.help_text: Children receiving Employment and Support Allowance or Universal Credit in their own right are not eligible

check_child_not_apprentice.question: Is the child NOT in a disqualifying apprenticeship?
check_child_not_apprentice.help_text: Regional rules apply: English apprenticeships (all types) disqualify. Scottish Modern Apprenticeships disqualify. Welsh Foundation Apprenticeships are APPROVED. Other Welsh apprenticeships disqualify.

check_claimant_residency.question: Does the claimant meet UK residency requirements?
check_claimant_residency.help_text: Must normally live in the UK or meet special circumstances
check_claimant_residency.evaluation_logic: If ANY path is satisfied, proceed to responsibility check

paths.requirement: Claimant normally lives in the UK

paths.description: Has settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
paths.requirement: Has settled status under EU Settlement Scheme

criteria: Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Working and earning/expect to earn above primary National Insurance threshold for 3 continuous months

paths.description: Pre-settled status and actively seeking work
criteria: Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Looking for work (can claim as jobseeker for up to 91 days)
Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Has sufficient resources to support self and family financially
NOT claiming Income Support, income-based JSA, Pension Credit, or Universal Credit
Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Studying and has sufficient resources to support self financially

paths.description: Pre-settled status as family member of EEA/Swiss national
criteria: Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Is family member of EEA or Swiss national with right to reside
One of: spouse/civil partner, dependent child/grandchild under 21, dependent parent/grandparent

paths.requirement: Living abroad as Crown servant or in certain qualifying countries

check_responsibility_for_child.question: Are you responsible for the child?
check_responsibility_for_child.help_text: Responsibility means living with child OR contributing to their upkeep
check_responsibility_for_child.evaluation_logic: If ANY path is satisfied, proceed to child location checks

criteria: You pay at least the same amount as Child Benefit (or equivalent in kind) towards their care
Contributions include: food, clothes, pocket money, housing, presents
Can be weekly, monthly, or lump sum payments

paths.description: Paying maintenance under court order or agreement
criteria: Payments made towards child's maintenance under court order or agreement
Payments cover the child's upkeep
Amount equals or exceeds Child Benefit payment

check_no_other_claimant.description: Check if another person is claiming Child Benefit for this child
check_no_other_claimant.routing_logic: If no other claimant, proceed. If other claimant exists and parties agree who should claim, route accordingly. If dispute exists, defer to HMRC for arbitration.

check_child_location.description: Determine if special rules apply based on child's location
check_child_location.routing_logic: Check if child is in hospital, care, fostered, or living normally

check_hospital_duration.question: Has the child's cumulative time in hospital or residential accommodation exceeded 12 weeks?
check_hospital_duration.help_text: Child Benefit continues for up to 12 weeks in hospital/residential accommodation. CUMULATIVE RULE: If child returns within 28 days of leaving, time counts towards 12-week limit. Multiple stays within 28 days are added together.

criteria.calculation_method: Sum all hospital/residential stays. If gap between stays is less than 28 days, count both stays towards cumulative total. If gap is 28+ days, reset counter.
criteria.example: 10 weeks hospital + 15 day gap + 3 weeks hospital = 13 weeks cumulative (exceeds limit)

check_regularly_spending_on_child_hospit.question: Are you regularly spending money on the child while they are in hospital/residential accommodation?
check_regularly_spending_on_child_hospit.help_text: Exception to 12-week rule if you regularly spend money on child. For hospital abroad, only if they went abroad for treatment and you're back in UK. Partner's spending counts if married/civil partnered and living together.

check_care_duration.question: Has the child been in care for 8 weeks or less, OR do they spend at least 24 hours a week at home?
check_care_duration.help_text: Child Benefit continues for up to 8 weeks in care. Exception if child spends 24+ hours/week at home. Can claim again if child spends 2 consecutive nights/week or 7+ days in a row at home.

check_fostering_arrangement.question: Is the local council NOT paying towards the child's accommodation or maintenance?
check_fostering_arrangement.help_text: You can claim Child Benefit for a fostered child if the council is not paying for their accommodation or maintenance

check_council_not_paying.question: Is the local council NOT paying towards the child's accommodation or maintenance?
check_council_not_paying.help_text: You generally cannot get Child Benefit if the local council is paying for the child

ELIGIBLE.description: Applicant meets all criteria for Child Benefit
ELIGIBLE.reason: Meets all Child Benefit eligibility requirements
next_steps: Apply online or by post using form CH2
Provide proof of identity for yourself and the child
Provide child's birth certificate or adoption certificate
Be aware of High Income Child Benefit Charge if you or partner earns over £60,000
Payments made every 4 weeks (or weekly if single parent or receiving certain benefits)

INELIGIBLE_no_qualifying_child.reason: No qualifying child under 16, or under 20 in approved education/training
INELIGIBLE_no_qualifying_child.guidance: Child must be under 16, or aged 16-19 and in approved education or training. If child just turned 20, benefit stops. If 16-17 year old leaves education, benefit can continue for 20 weeks if they register with careers service or armed services.

INELIGIBLE_child_working_full_time.reason: Child aged 16+ is working 24 or more hours per week and not in approved education or training
INELIGIBLE_child_working_full_time.guidance: Child Benefit stops if child aged 16 or over starts paid work for 24+ hours per week and is no longer in approved education or training. If they return to education/training, you may be able to claim again.

INELIGIBLE_child_receiving_benefits.reason: Child is receiving certain benefits in their own right
INELIGIBLE_child_receiving_benefits.guidance: Child Benefit stops if child starts receiving Employment and Support Allowance, Universal Credit, or similar benefits in their own right.

INELIGIBLE_child_apprentice.reason: Child has started an apprenticeship in England
INELIGIBLE_child_apprentice.guidance: Child Benefit stops if child starts an apprenticeship in England.

INELIGIBLE_residency_requirements.reason: Does not meet UK residency requirements
INELIGIBLE_residency_requirements.guidance: Must normally live in the UK or meet special circumstances (settled status, pre-settled status with qualifying criteria, Crown servant, or living in certain countries). If you have pre-settled status, you may need to be working, studying, or have sufficient resources.

INELIGIBLE_not_responsible.guidance: You must be responsible for the child, which means either living with them OR paying at least the Child Benefit amount towards their care (food, clothes, housing, pocket money, etc.). This can include maintenance payments under court order.

INELIGIBLE_hospital_over_12_weeks.reason: Child has been in hospital or residential accommodation for more than 12 weeks and you are not regularly spending money on them
INELIGIBLE_hospital_over_12_weeks.guidance: Child Benefit stops after 12 weeks in hospital or residential accommodation, unless you regularly spend money on them. If child returns within 28 days of leaving, the time counts towards the 12-week limit. Contact HMRC to check if exceptions apply. Partner's spending only counts if married/civil partnered and living together.

INELIGIBLE_care_over_8_weeks.reason: Child has been in local authority care for more than 8 weeks and does not spend at least 24 hours a week at home
INELIGIBLE_care_over_8_weeks.guidance: Child Benefit stops after 8 weeks in care, unless child spends at least 24 hours a week at home. You can claim again if child spends 2 consecutive nights each week or 7+ days in a row at home. Contact HMRC about your specific situation.

INELIGIBLE_council_paying_fostering.reason: Local council is paying towards the child's accommodation or maintenance (fostering)
INELIGIBLE_council_paying_fostering.guidance: You can claim Child Benefit for a fostered child only if the local council is NOT paying towards their accommodation or maintenance. Check with the council about their payment arrangements.

INELIGIBLE_council_paying.reason: Local council is paying towards the child's accommodation or maintenance
INELIGIBLE_council_paying.guidance: You generally cannot get Child Benefit if the local council is paying towards the child's accommodation or maintenance. This applies to informal care arrangements and some fostering situations.

INELIGIBLE_advanced_education.reason: Child aged 16-19 is in advanced education (university degree, HNC, HND, NVQ Level 4+)
INELIGIBLE_advanced_education.guidance: Child Benefit stops when child starts advanced education such as university degrees, Higher National Certificates (HNC), Higher National Diplomas (HND), or NVQ Level 4 and above. Approved education for Child Benefit is limited to non-advanced courses like A-levels, T-levels, Scottish Highers, and NVQ Level 3 or below.

INELIGIBLE_part_time_education.reason: Child aged 16-19 is in part-time education (less than 12 hours per week supervised study)
INELIGIBLE_part_time_education.guidance: Child Benefit for 16-19 year olds requires full-time approved education, defined as at least 12 hours per week of supervised study. Part-time courses do not qualify. If child increases hours to 12+ per week, you may be able to claim.

INELIGIBLE_employer_funded_training.reason: Child aged 16-19 is in employer-funded training or training with employer agreement for future job
INELIGIBLE_employer_funded_training.guidance: Training courses funded by an employer or courses taken with an agreement for future employment with the employer do not count as approved education or training for Child Benefit purposes.

INELIGIBLE_extension_not_applicable.reason: 20-week extension not applicable (child not registered with qualifying service, extension period expired, or child turned 18)
INELIGIBLE_extension_not_applicable.guidance: The 20-week extension for 16-17 year olds who leave education is only available if they register with a government careers service, Connexions service, or armed services. The extension ends after 20 weeks or when the child turns 18, whichever is sooner. If extension has expired and child is not in approved education/training, Child Benefit stops.

DEFERRED_TO_HMRC.reason: Multiple people claim responsibility for the child and cannot agree who should claim Child Benefit
DEFERRED_TO_HMRC.guidance: When two or more people are responsible for a child and cannot agree who should claim, HMRC will make the decision. HMRC considers: (1) Who the child lives with most of the time (primary residence), (2) Who contributes most to the child's upkeep, (3) The child's best interests, (4) Continuity of existing arrangements. Both parties should contact the Child Benefit Office with evidence of their circumstances. HMRC's decision is binding. Common scenarios: separated parents with shared custody, child living part-time with each parent, grandparent vs parent both contributing.
hmrc_decision_criteria: Primary residence (where child spends most nights)
Main contributor to child's upkeep and care
Stability and continuity for the child
required_evidence: Proof of residence (utility bills, rental agreement)
Court orders or formal custody arrangements (if applicable)

age_limits.birthday_rule: Benefit stops on child's 20th birthday, not end of academic year

training_exclusions: Training with employer agreement for future job

payment_rates.note: Current rates not specified in source documents - check gov.uk for latest rates

high_income_charge.note: High Income Child Benefit Charge applies if you or partner earns over £60,000. This is a payment adjustment, not an eligibility criterion.

age_and_education.rule: Child must be under 16, OR aged 16-19 and in approved education or training
age_and_education.approved_education: Full-time non-advanced education (12+ hours/week supervised study): A-levels, T-levels, Scottish Highers, NVQ Level 3 or below
age_and_education.advanced_education_excluded: University degrees, HNC (Level 4), HND (Level 5), NVQ Level 4+, professional qualifications Level 4+
age_and_education.minimum_hours: 12 hours per week supervised study required for 16-19 year olds
age_and_education.approved_training: Unpaid work-based training with education provider, or approved training course. Employer-funded training excluded.
age_and_education.extension: If 16-17 year old leaves education/training, benefit continues for 20 weeks if registered with careers service, Connexions, or armed services. Ends at week 20 or age 18, whichever sooner.
age_and_education.birthday_cutoff: Benefit stops on child's 20th birthday, not at end of academic year
age_and_education.married_children: Married 16-19 year olds only qualify if financially dependent on claimant
ending_conditions: Child starts paid work 24+ hours per week and leaves education/training
Child receives ESA, Universal Credit, or similar benefits in own right
Child starts advanced education (university, HNC, HND)

apprenticeship_rules.england: ALL apprenticeships disqualify (Intermediate, Advanced, Higher, Degree)
apprenticeship_rules.wales: Foundation Apprenticeships APPROVED. Other Welsh apprenticeships disqualify.
apprenticeship_rules.note: Welsh Foundation Apprenticeships are the ONLY apprenticeships that qualify for Child Benefit

residency.rule: Must normally live in UK or meet special circumstances
residency.settled_status: Can claim with settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
presettled_status_conditions: Working and earning above NI primary threshold for 3 continuous months, OR
Looking for work (jobseeker for up to 91 days), OR
Sufficient resources (not claiming IS, JSA, PC, UC), OR
Family member of EEA/Swiss national with right to reside
residency.abroad: May qualify if Crown servant or living in certain countries

responsibility.rule: Must be responsible for child through living with them OR contributing to upkeep
responsibility.contributing: Must pay at least Child Benefit amount (or equivalent in kind) towards care
responsibility.contribution_threshold: £26.05 per week for eldest/only child, £17.25 per week for additional children (2024/25 rates - check gov.uk for current rates)
responsibility.frequency: Can be weekly, monthly, or lump sum
responsibility.combined_contributions: Two people can combine contributions if both contribute to same child

unequal_allocation.rule: When contributing to multiple children, can allocate contribution amounts unequally
unequal_allocation.explanation: If contributing £40 for two children, can allocate as £26.05 (eldest) + £13.95 (second), or any split where each child receives at least their applicable rate
unequal_allocation.example_1: £40 total for 2 children: £26.05 (child 1) + £13.95 (child 2) = eligible for child 1 only
unequal_allocation.example_2: £50 total for 2 children: £26.05 (child 1) + £23.95 (child 2) = eligible for both
unequal_allocation.note: Each child must receive at least the Child Benefit rate they would generate (eldest rate for first child, additional rate for others)

responsibility.adoption_allowance: Adoption allowance does NOT disqualify (unlike fostering allowance)

priority_rules.rule: Only one person can claim Child Benefit for a child
priority_rules.multiple_carers: If two people are responsible, they must agree who claims

hmrc_arbitration.rule: If agreement cannot be reached, HMRC decides based on evidence
criteria: Primary residence (where child spends most nights per week)
Main contributor to child's upkeep and care
Day-to-day care responsibilities
Stability and continuity for the child
Existing claim history
evidence_required: Proof of residence (utility bills, rental agreement, council tax)
Financial contribution evidence (bank statements, receipts, maintenance records)
Court orders or formal custody arrangements (if applicable)
Statements from child's school, doctor, or other professionals
common_scenarios: Separated parents with child spending 4 days vs 3 days per week with each
Grandparent caring for grandchild while parent works
Child living part-time with two different households
Parent vs step-parent both contributing significantly
hmrc_arbitration.appeal_process: Contact Child Benefit Office if circumstances change significantly

priority_rules.transfer: Current claimant can transfer by contacting Child Benefit Office
priority_rules.child_moves: If child moves but you still contribute to upkeep (at least CB amount), you can continue claiming

hospital.time_limit: 12 weeks in hospital or residential accommodation
hospital.linking_rule: CUMULATIVE: If child returns within 28 days, time counts towards 12-week limit. Multiple stays within 28 days add together.
hospital.example: 10 weeks + 15 day gap + 3 weeks = 13 weeks cumulative (exceeds limit)
hospital.exception: Can continue if regularly spending money on child
hospital.abroad: For hospital abroad, only if went for treatment and you're back in UK spending on them
hospital.mental_health: Psychiatric units count as hospital, not custody

care.exception_24_hours: Can continue if child spends 24+ hours per week at home
care.exception_nights: Can continue if child spends 2 consecutive nights per week at home
care.reclaim_conditions: Can claim again if child spends 2 consecutive nights/week OR 7+ consecutive days at home
care.custody: Youth custody counts as care, 8-week limit applies

hospital_and_care.partner_spending: Partner's money only counts if married/civil partnered and living together

council_payments.rule: Generally cannot claim if local council is paying for child's accommodation or maintenance
council_payments.fostering: Can claim for fostered child only if council NOT paying
council_payments.informal_care: Cannot claim if council paying for informal care arrangement

administrative.backdating: Claims can be backdated up to 3 months

high_income_charge.rule: HICBC is a payment adjustment, not an eligibility criterion. Claimant is still eligible but may owe tax.
high_income_charge.calculation: Tax charge = 1% of Child Benefit for every £200 of income between £60k-£80k
high_income_charge.who_pays: Higher earner in household pays, regardless of who claims Child Benefit
examples: Income £80,000+: 100% clawback (but still eligible, just repay full amount)
high_income_charge.opt_out: Can choose not to receive payments to avoid charge, but should still register to protect National Insurance credits
high_income_charge.partner_income: Partner's income applies even if they are not the claimant. Higher earner always pays.
high_income_charge.not_eligibility: Important: HICBC does NOT affect eligibility. It is a post-payment tax charge only.

payment_structure.eldest_child_rate: Higher rate for first child claimed
payment_structure.additional_children_rate: Lower rate for each additional child
payment_structure.split_family_rule: If two parents each claim for different children from same family, both get eldest child rate for their first child
payment_structure.example: Divorced parents: Mother claims for child A (gets eldest rate), Father claims for child B (also gets eldest rate, not additional rate)
payment_structure.frequency: Paid every 4 weeks, or weekly if single parent or receiving certain benefits
